title: Add --output-format json and --json-schema for structured agent responses
source: https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code/sdk - Claude Code structured output via --json-schema flag
description: The current system relies on fragile regex parsing of markdown (parse_agent_output at delegate.py:615) to extract status, files_changed, and description from agent responses. Claude Code now supports --output-format json and --json-schema for validated structured output. By passing a JSON schema, the CLI guarantees the response conforms to the expected format — eliminating regex failures, Dutch/English fallback parsing, and the 'last 500 chars' fallback when no structured summary is found.
proposed_changes: 1. Define a JSON schema for agent responses: {status: string, files_changed: string[], description: string, open_questions: string, team_updates?: {messages: [], decisions: [], blocked_on: []}}. Save as scripts/schemas/agent_response.json. 2. In delegate_to_agent() (line 695), add '--output-format', 'json' and '--json-schema', json.dumps(schema) to the cmd list. 3. Replace parse_agent_output() regex parsing (lines 615-670) with json.loads(result.stdout)['result'] to extract the structured response directly. 4. Keep the regex parser as a fallback for backward compatibility during transition. 5. Similarly update parse_team_agent_output() to use a team-aware schema that includes messages_to_team, decisions_made, and blocked_on fields.
